name: Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup
section_which_is_best_for_you:
In this article, we are looking at three major Local SEO tools that can help with end-to-end listing management, reputation management, social media, and making sure you’re ranking on SERPs. 

We are going to delve deep into each of these tools to understand their functionalities better, looking at the pros and cons and talking in detail about their pricing structure as well. 

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: At a glance!

Birdeye is designed to help small businesses manage their online reputation, focusing primarily on reviews. However, it doesn't use its technology for listings management and instead relies on third-party vendors for its tech. Birdeye's pricing structure, ranging from $200 to $300 per location per month, is quite high and not suitable for partners. This makes it less competitive compared to other, more affordable options in the market.

Yext on the other hand, offers an end-to-end local SEO toolkit and does everything right from listings to store locator pages. It also offers integration and partnership programs for agencies, consultants, and other SaaS resellers/platforms, however, the support after partnering is limited. It's mostly enterprise-focused and, with its customer support being below sub-par, its pricing is not justified.

Synup offers comprehensive Local SEO tools and features to help you rank better on SERPs, right from everything to listings, reputation, social media management to local analytics, keyword and grid rank tracking, and more. It is also completely API and AI-enabled, which makes it much more efficient. Additionally, it also offers Managed Services for outsourcing tasks and robust white-labeling options, enabling agencies to create custom-branded portals and deliver a personalized experience that builds client trust.
section_listing_management:
Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Listings Management 

Birdeye’s listing management software helps manage your listings across Google, Apple Maps, Bing, Facebook and across other search engines. It comes with a centralized dashboard that helps you view all your listings, add rich text such as images, links, social posts, appointment links and more. It also helps analyze keywords and listing trends that helps you optimize your profile and SEO investments. There is also an option to audit your listings, benchmark them against your competitors and identify gaps to fix them. However, customers point out that there have been issues with integrations and the overall listing management experience is not as seamless. 

Yext has a broader network of over 100 publishers, making it well-suited for large-scale listings management. It supports bulk updates, allowing you to manage numerous listings at once. However, a common complaint among customers is the loss of access or ownership of their listings after discontinuing Yext's services. Additionally, Yext has been criticized for its ineffective handling of duplicate listings, often failing to suppress them adequately. While its expansive network is a significant advantage, these issues highlight potential drawbacks to consider.

Synup provides an all-in-one listings management system that connects with over 60 publishers, including Google, Facebook, and Bing. It's fully API-based, allows easy integration and bulk verification of multiple locations. You maintain full control and ownership of your data, ensuring you won’t lose your listings if you change platforms. Synup automatically updates your business information across directories, making it as seamless as possible. Plus, it includes a voice-optimizer feature that integrates with popular voice assistants like Siri, Google Assistant, Alexa, and Cortana.

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Reputation Management


Birdeye comes with an interactive reputation management dashboard and oversees reputation management across 200+ websites and online directories, it provides a seamless review management experience to its users, which is hands down the best in the industry. It enables you to monitor, respond and generate more reviews via the platform. 

It also comes with built-in AI review response features which help you respond to replies quickly and tailor them as per your needs. It also has in-built features that lets you collect reviews in real-time from your customers using a QR code which makes it really easy for people to leave you reviews online. However, majority of customer say that it’s pricing is too high and not justified when it comes to the platform as a whole.

Yext offers a robust reputation management system with features like Generative Review Response, first-party review generation, and an interactive dashboard. However, many potential users are deterred by Yext's high fees and impersonal customer support.

Synup’s review management system offers customization and simplicity. Its AI-driven response generator helps you create consistent, relevant replies quickly. Customizable templates allow you to align responses with your brand's voice, while the smart response system streamlines the review management process. The platform is affordably priced, making it ideal for agencies, resellers, and SMBs looking to enhance their reputation management. Users also appreciate Synup's intuitive interface and frequent monthly updates, which keep the system current and user-friendly.

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Social Media Management 

Birdeye has an integrated dashboard for social media management that helps generate posts, hashtags, copies with the help of AI capabilities (Bird AI) and helps get contextually relevant social media images based on certain prompts. With their collaborative capabilities, it makes it a seamless experience altogether. However, due to its pricing, users tend to turn to competitors for managing their social media profiles.

Synup is the most cost-effective social media management tool for agencies, featuring advanced AI and a full agency-enabled platform. It allows you to create, publish, schedule, and monitor content across various social media channels and includes integrated social listening tools for real-time insights. 

Yext’s social tool comes with an intuitive dashboard, along with additional AI capabilities. It also comes with baked-in analytics for social media reporting and insights that can help you understand your performance. It comes with cross-team collaboration tools that can help you carry out conversations easily and make sure that everyone is on the same page. Keeping this in mind, it’d be worthy to note that users don’t prefer Yext for social media management due to their pricing structure and inconsistencies. 

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Local SEO 

Birdeye does not offer store locators, however, it does offer search pages that help businesses showcase information such as directions, business hours and online reviews that are brand-customized.

Yext provides a suite of features for enterprises and large-scale businesses. This includes a Rank Tracker to keep an eye on search engine rankings, customizable Store Locator Pages to help customers find locations, and an AI-powered chatbot for landing pages to help with user engagement. 

Synup provides Local SEO tools for businesses, including a keyword rank tracker, grid rank tracker, store locator pages. It also enables hyper-localized campaigns, triggering promotions based on events like weather changes or holidays.

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Local Analytics 

Birdeye helps businesses analyze how they rank on local map for their target location for specific keywords, along with a free local SEO audit tool. However, people say that they provide limited capabilities when it comes to granular location level tracking. 

Synup excels in essential local analytics, including tracking profile views, calls, website visits, and local rankings with keyword and grid rank tools. It offers comprehensive coverage in these areas. However, as we discussed, the platform's reporting and visualization could use some improvement.

Yext provides SEO and listing analytics for enterprise but lacks local analytics like location-level insights or rank tracking, which are crucial for local businesses.

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Reseller Support 

Birdeye does not provide whitelabeling capabilities, however, it has recently introduced managed services that helps businesses with taking over their listings, reputation and their overall local SEO strategy. However, since it’s majorly targeting large companies, their pricing module does not suit SMBs and small scale businesses. 

Yext offers integration and partnership program for agencies and consultants as well as other SaaS resellers/platforms. However, the support you get once you partner with them is minismal and given their focus on enterprise segment, they don’t invest as much into growing their partner’s business.

Synup stands out due to its exceptional support for agencies and resellers. Unlike Yext and Birdeye, which offer basic reselling solutions, Synup provides a comprehensive platform that not only facilitates reselling but also helps agencies manage clients, generate leads, and streamline operations. It essentially functions as agency operations software. Additionally, Synup includes a white-label business scan tool that can be integrated into your site to capture leads at prices that do not break your bank. 

Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: Which One Should You Choose?

If you’re a mid-sized or a large company and do not mind spending on your reputation management, then Birdeye is the tool you can go for! With managed services and listing management capabilities, it is a decent choice for mid and large sized companies. 

And, if you’re an enterprise who’s looking for a seamless experience when it comes to local SEO, you can look at exploring Yext’s services. But do note, that customer support might not always be top-notch and there might be a few features that one may be missing out on

With Synup, you benefit from exceptional customer support and budget-friendly, customized packages tailored to your needs. It also offers comprehensive managed services and full white-labeling options, covering everything from listings management to grid rank tracking. This helps you streamline your operations and enhance your agency's visibility, ensuring you stand out in a competitive market.

‍

‍

Summing it Up

We’ve provided a transparent overview of how each tool performs, highlighting their unique strengths and limitations. Based on your specific needs, we recommend selecting the tool that best fits your segment and trying it out to see how well it works for you.

‍
faq_rich_text:
Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup: FAQs

  1. Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup reviews

Birdeye 

  • Pros: Excellent reputation management, customer experience tools, and integrations. 
  • Cons: Higher cost and complex setup.

Yext

  • Pros: Strong in listings management, knowledge graph, and all-in-one features.
  • Cons: Expensive and below sub-par customer support feedback.

Synup

  • Pros: Effective local listings management, detailed analytics, and managed services.
  • Cons: Less intuitive interface. 

‍

  1. Birdeye v/s Podium

Birdeye offers a comprehensive solution for reputation management, customer feedback, and communication, including review management, surveys, and analytics, while, Podium focuses on streamlined customer communication through messaging and reviews, with added features for surveys and payments both are online reputation management softwares. 

‍

  1. Birdeye v/s Yext v/s Synup cost

Birdeye costs $299 for it’s Starter pack, $399 for its Growth pack and $499 for it’s Dominate pack. This is mostly paid up annually. 

Yext charges $4, $9, $10, and $19 per week for their packages which include different features. However, these prices are not consistent and are known to be extremely expensive. 

Synup charges a standard of $35 Per Month for its standard package and $90 Per Month for its premium package. Synup Social on the other hand has different costs attached to it. These prices can be customized for businesses, based on their size and the amount of locations they are targeting. It is advisable to contact them directly for customized plans. 

‍

seo.description: Looking for a tool that helps you stand out amongst other digital agencies? We’ve got an in-depth guide that will help you choose the right tool for your business.
